ac2_noCanvas2D.evidence: grep for Canvas2D/getContext('2d') returns zero hits in src/

ac4_gracefulFallback.evidence: No WebGL detection; Three.js throws uncaught error if WebGL unavailable

step1_webglDetection.notes: Adds a guard function and custom error class to scene.ts. Minimal surface area change. The initScene return type and signature remain unchanged for callers where WebGL is available.

step2_fallbackUI.notes: Requires wrapping the top-level initScene() call in main.ts with try/catch. Currently initScene is called at module scope (line 26), so it must be moved into an async IIFE or guarded block. This is a minor but necessary structural change to main.ts.

concerns.description: initScene() is currently called at module top-level in main.ts (line 26). Wrapping it in try/catch requires restructuring main.ts to move all dependent code into a guarded block or function. The plan should explicitly address this restructuring since renderer, scene, camera, and all downstream code depend on the initScene result.
concerns.recommendation: Wrap the entire main.ts body in an init() function, with the WebGL catch at the top returning early before any dependent setup runs.
